|gate= $6,720,000<ref name="gate&att"/>

'''''UFC 304: Edwards vs. Muhammad 2''''' was a mixed martial arts event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on July 27, 2024, at the [[Co-op Live]] in [[Manchester]], England.<ref name="Manchester">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/24/24138996/ufc-304-will-return-to-manchester-this-summer|title=UFC 304 announced for Manchester this summer|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guillherme Cruz|date=2024-04-24|access-date=2024-04-24}}</ref>

==Background==
[[File:The Co-Op Live Arena (geograph 7796386).jpg|thumb|left|This was the first sporting event to take place at the [[Co-op Live]] arena, which opened in April 2024.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/articles/c80z4epzd13o.amp|title=UFC 304 confirmed for Manchester in July|publisher=bbc.com|author=Staff|date=2024-04-24|access-date=2024-04-24}}</ref>]] 

The event marked the promotion's fifth visit to Manchester and first since [[UFC 204]] in October 2016.<ref name="Manchester"/> Despite the event taking place in Manchester, the event was held based on [[Pacific Time Zone|Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7)]] (Las Vegas) time with preliminary fights starting at 23.00 British Summer Time (15.00 PDT), and the main card starting 03.00 BST, which is the standard start time for UFC pay-per-view events of 19.00 PDT, which drew massive criticism from fans and fighters.  Fans believed the matches should have started earlier (19.00 BST, or 11.00 PDT) for the UK audience.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Mathew Riddle|date=2024-05-01|title=Aspinall: UFC 304 Start Time 'Absolutely Terrible'|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/ufc/ufc-304-tom-aspinall-start-time|access-date=2024-07-28|publisher=si.com}}</ref>

A [[UFC Welterweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Leon Edwards]] and [[Belal Muhammad]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-05-16|title=UFC 304 official with Leon Edwards vs. Belal Muhammad headliner and more in Manchester|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-304-manchester-england-edwards-muhammad-heavyweight|access-date=2024-05-16|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com}}</ref> The pair previously fought at [[UFC Fight Night: Edwards vs. Muhammad]] in March 2021, where the fight was declared a no contest following an accidental eye poke which left Muhammad unable to continue.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-03-14|title=UFC Fight Night 187 results: Leon Edwards vs. Belal Muhammad ends in nasty accidental eye poke|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/03/ufc-fight-night-187-results-leon-edwards-vs-belal-muhammad-eye-poke-no-contest|access-date=2024-05-16|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref>

An interim [[UFC Heavyweight Championship]] bout between the title holder [[Tom Aspinall]] and [[Curtis Blaydes]] co-headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-05-16|title=Interim heavyweight champ Tom Aspinall vs. Curtis Blaydes set for UFC 304 co-main event|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-304-tom-aspinall-vs-curtis-blaydes-interim-heavyweight-title-co-main-event-manchester-england|access-date=2024-05-16|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff}}</ref> They previously met at [[UFC Fight Night: Blaydes vs. Aspinall]] in July 2022 in which Blaydes won after Aspinall injured his knee fifteen seconds into the bout keeping him out of competition for a year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2022/07/23/ufc-london-curtis-blaydes-vs-tom-aspinall/|title=UFC London: Aspinall suffers knee injury 15 seconds in, Blaydes takes TKO decision|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Eddie Law|date=2022-07-23|access-date=2024-05-16}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[Christian Leroy Duncan]] and [[Robert Bryczek]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Bryson Hester|date=2024-05-23|title=UFC 304 adds Christian Leroy Duncan vs. Robert Bryczek|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/05/23/ufc-304-adds-christian-leroy-duncan-vs-robert-bryczek/|access-date=2024-05-24|publisher=cagesidepress.com}}</ref> However, Bryczek withdrew from the fight for unknown reasons and was replaced by former [[Legacy Fighting Alliance#LFA Middleweight Championship|LFA Middleweight Champion]] [[Gregory Rodrigues]].<ref name="Rodrigues">{{Cite news|title=UFC 304: savage middleweight strikers clash at Manchester PPV|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/ufc-304-savage-middleweight-strikers-clash-at-manchester-ppv|publisher=si.com|author=Mathew Riddle|access-date=2024-06-28}}</ref>

A women's strawweight bout between Ravena Oliveira and Shauna Bannon was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Parviz Iskenderov|date=2024-05-14|title=UFC 304: Molly McCann faces Bruna Brasil, Shauna Bannon meets Ravena Oliveira|url=https://www.fightmag.com/ufc-304-molly-mccann-faces-bruna-brasil-shauna-bannon-meets-ravena-oliveira/|access-date=2024-05-15|publisher=fightmag.com}}</ref> However, Oliveira withdrew from the bout due to an injury and was replaced with promotional newcomer Alice Ardelean.<ref name="aliceshauna">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/7/15/24198823/tiktok-star-alice-ardelean-added-to-ufc-304-in-manchester|title=TikTok star Alice Ardelean added to UFC 304 fight card in Manchester|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Ryan Harkness|date=2024-07-15|access-date=2024-07-15}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between Ramon Taveras and Caolán Loughran was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Ian O'Neill|date=2024-04-17|title=Caolan Loughran vs. Ramon Tavares set for UFC 304|url=https://severemma.com/2024/05/caolan-loughran-vs-ramon-tavares-set-for-ufc-304/|access-date=2024-04-17|publisher=severemma.com}}</ref> However, Taveras had to withdraw from the bout due to an injury and was reportedly replaced by Hecher Sosa.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/07/ufc-304-ramon-taveras-vs-caolan-loughran-manchester-injury|title=Ramon Taveras withdraws from UFC 304 vs. Caolan Loughran: 'It sucks that it came to this'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-07-17|access-date=2024-07-17}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=New Spanish partner for Topuria: Hecher joins the UFC|url=https://as.com/masdeporte/polideportivo/nuevo-companero-espanol-para-topuria-hecher-da-el-salto-a-la-ufc-n/?omnil=resrelrecomv|date=2024-07-18|access-date=2024-07-18|publisher=as.com|author=Daniel Manso}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> It was later reported that Sosa would not be competing at the event as Loughran would face Jake Hadley instead.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Hecher Sosa's dream on pause: "It was very close!"|url=https://as.com/masdeporte/polideportivo/el-sueno-de-hecher-sosa-en-pausa-falto-muy-poco-n/|date=2024-07-18|access-date=2024-07-19|publisher=as.com|author=Cristina Parra}} {{in lang|es}}</ref><ref name="taverasout">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-bellator-pfl-news-matchup-roundup-fight-bookings-schedule-july-22-2024|title=Matchup Roundup: New UFC, PFL, Bellator fights announced in the past week (July 15-21)|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-07-22|access-date=2024-07-22}}</ref> At the weigh-ins, Hadley weighed in at 137 pounds, half a pound over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and Hadley was fined 20 percent of his purse which went to Loughran.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/7/26/24206946/ufc-304-weigh-in-results-2-title-bouts-official-1-fighter-misses|title=UFC 304 weigh-in results: 2 title bouts official, 1 fighter misses|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2024-07-26|access-date=2024-07-26}}</ref>

The event originally featured $50,000 fight night bonuses, which were raised to $100,000 before the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/07/ufc-304-bonuses-100k-king-green-dana-white-manchester|title=Dana White increases UFC 304 performance bonuses to six figures after King Green's plea|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King, Ken Hathaway and Abbey Subhan|date=2024-07-25|access-date=2024-07-28}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bjpenn.com/mma-news/ufc-304/ufc-304-bonus-report-paddy-pimblett-takes-home-200k/|title=UFC 304 Bonus Report: Paddy Pimblett takes home $200k|publisher=bjpenn.com|author=Chris Taylor|date=2024-07-27|access-date=2024-07-27}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night:''' No bonus awarded.
*'''Performance of the Night ($200,000): Paddy Pimblett'''
*'''Performance of the Night ($100,000): Tom Aspinall''' and '''Mick Parkin'''
